frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: Information processing minimizes perceptual error under capacity constraints.

frameworks.name: universal law of generalization
frameworks.one_line: Exponential decay of generalization over psychological-space distance.

core_question: Why does perceptual generalization follow an exponential function of psychological distance?
key_assumptions: Systems minimize cost of perceptual error.
Generalization can be formulated in a 'psychological space'.
key_findings: The exponential universal law emerges from any system minimizing perceptual error under information constraints.
Result holds for both biological and artificial information-processing systems.
interpretations: Universal law of generalization is a consequence of efficient coding, not prior theoretical accounts.
Generalization behavior reflects information-theoretic optimization rather than associative or geometric assumptions.
